comments.text: The financial markets have been unpredictable lately. Even long-term investing doesn’t feel as straightforward as it used to. I came into trading with high expectations, but the reality has been a lot more inconsistent than I expected. I’ve tried different approaches but nothing feels stable yet...

Getting into trading changed my perspective completely. What looked simple from a distance turned into a challenge filled with unpredictable price action and \\\.conflicting information. I’ve spent time trying different methods, but I still haven’t found a strategy that makes sense for me. Watching other traders celebrate their wins while I struggle is tough. Is there someone who actually teaches the process step by step rather than repeating the same common advice?

I was stuck in that same cycle until I found Steven Cavagnaro. He’s the only mentor I’ve seen who breaks down 1-minute scalping with structure, not just flashy wins. He teaches exact entry timing, stop placement, and risk control  so you’re not just guessing.

Finally stopped overtrading after 2 years lol. it’s crazy how much my pnl changed just by waiting for the right liquidity sweeps like Rolando Mario taught me. if you're still staring at charts 10 hours a day and losing, you're doing it wrong. quality over quantity every time.

What account size do you trade? I calculated the fees for trading 4h-1h timeframes with 1% risk per trade and the fees are insane. I'd need a very high R:R and winrate to be profitable with a small account.

Trading advice for anyone new to crypto: try not to focus too much on the 1-minute charts. When I first started trading, I made the mistake of reacting to every small price movement, and it cost me a lot of money. Constantly watching the smallest timeframes can create unnecessary stress and lead to impulsive decisions.
